Title: Isolation, biological activity, and synthesis of isoquinoline alkaloids

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as an entry enzyme for stereoselective in vitro and microbial production of natural and unnatural benzylisoquinoline alkaloids; can be combined with upstream dopamine/aldehyde-generating enzymes in one-pot cascades and engineered for expanded substrate scope.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the Pictet–Spengler condensation of dopamine or dopamine derivatives with aldehydes to form chiral tetrahydroisoquinoline/benzylisoquinoline scaffolds such as norcoclaurine-type products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables efficient, highly stereoselective biosynthesis of natural and unnatural benzylisoquinoline alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro multienzyme cascade biocatalysis; product isolation; stereochemical analysis; substrate scope testing; mutant analysis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Committed entry step forming core scaffold of target alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzymatic reaction conditions including pH and temperature were emphasized as tunable factors.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CnTYR; EfTyrDC; CvTAm; BLPad; AnPad; StyAB; RoStyC; Cj-6-OMT; RnCOMT; MxSafC
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review summarizes TfNCS from Thalictrum flavum as unusually tolerant to α-methyl-substituted aldehydes and, in some variants, ketones, enabling access to corydaline-like and spiro-THIQ products difficult by classical synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Substrate scope remains enzyme-dependent; some substrates require engineered variants for improved conversion or diastereoselectivity.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Dopamine derivatives; aryl/aliphatic aldehydes; in some engineered systems ketones
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TfNCS is a core entry enzyme for benzylisoquinoline alkaloid biosynthesis. 2. In vitro cascade systems using TfNCS produced natural and unnatural benzylisoquinolines with high stereoselectivity. 3. Engineered/wild-type TfNCS variants broadened acceptance to α-methyl aldehydes and even ketones, enabling corydaline-like and spiro-THIQ products.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmacologically active natural products and synthetic biology intermediates

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be paired with decarboxylases, transaminases, and NCS to generate upstream catechol or dopamine-derived intermediates for benzylisoquinoline production; useful for halogenated analog synthesis after mutagenesis.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes tyrosine or halogenated tyrosine derivatives to catechol intermediates that feed multienzyme benzylisoquinoline biosynthesis cascades.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies catechol precursor chemistry enabling one-pot synthesis of natural and unnatural benzylisoquinolines, including halogenated variants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro multienzyme cascade assays; mutant analysis; product isolation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutagenesis improved tolerance to halogenated tyrosines for halobenzylisoquinoline synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Native substrate tolerance can limit halogenated analog production without engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CnTYR provides oxidized tyrosine-derived precursors in one-pot benzylisoquinoline cascades. 2. It supported synthesis of natural and unnatural benzylisoquinolines. 3. Mutant tyrosinase broadened use to halogenated substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive alkaloid scaffolds and synthetic intermediates

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream enzyme in one-pot cascades to generate tyramine-like or dopamine-related aldehyde/amine precursor supply for benzylisoquinoline biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates tyrosine derivatives in precursor-forming cascades feeding benzylisoquinoline biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes upstream precursor formation enabling multienzyme production of benzylisoquinolines.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EfTyrDC was part of upstream enzyme modules used for one-pot benzylisoquinoline formation. 2. It helped channel tyrosine derivatives toward NCS-compatible substrates.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be incorporated into one-pot systems to generate aldehyde intermediates for NCS-dependent THIQ formation; useful for modular precursor diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transaminates aldehyde/amine precursor intermediates in multienzyme cascades leading to benzylisoquinoline biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports efficient generation of aldehyde-compatible precursor pools for THIQ formation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CvTAm functioned in modular precursor generation for NCS-based THIQ synthesis. 2. It expanded flexibility of the in vitro benzylisoquinoline cascade.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Various microorganisms (in vitro enzyme systems; E. coli modules)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for constructing whole-cell cascades from coumaric acid derivatives toward benzylisoquinoline alkaloids; compatible with lignocellulosic feedstock-based systems.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates phenylpropanoid/coumaric acid derivatives in four-enzyme whole-cell cascades that generate aldehydes for NCS-dependent THIQ formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled efficient synthesis of natural isoquinoline alkaloids and derivatives from coumaric acid derivatives and dopamine derivatives in engineered E. coli.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Whole-cell E. coli biocatalytic cascade; product titer measurement.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Whole-cell biocatalysis; lignocellulosic biomass-derived feedstocks were highlighted.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review states target compound concentration could exceed 1 g L−1 in the whole-cell route.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Coumaric acid to aldehyde precursor module
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BLPad/AnPad initiated E. coli whole-cell cascades from coumaric acid derivatives. 2. These enzymes supported gram-per-liter scale production of isoquinoline alkaloids and derivatives when combined with downstream modules.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive alkaloid products and sustainable biomanufacturing targets

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be combined with decarboxylase, isomerase, and NCS in whole-cell E. coli to convert styrene-like intermediates to aldehydes for alkaloid synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Epoxidizes styrene-like intermediates in engineered whole-cell cascades, enabling subsequent isomerization to aldehydes used for THIQ formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supported efficient E. coli synthesis of natural isoquinoline alkaloids and derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. StyAB formed epoxide intermediates in E. coli whole-cell cascades. 2. Together with RoStyC and NCS it enabled downstream THIQ formation.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful in whole-cell cascades to generate aldehydes from epoxides, improving access to NCS substrates from simple aromatic feedstocks.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Isomerizes epoxide intermediates to aldehydes that serve as NCS substrates in isoquinoline alkaloid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled efficient whole-cell E. coli production of isoquinoline alkaloids and derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RoStyC generated aldehyde intermediates from epoxides in whole-cell E. coli cascades. 2. This enabled NCS-dependent isoquinoline alkaloid formation from simple feedstocks.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used for regioselective methylation in modular one-pot cascades to diversify THIQ scaffolds; useful in producing methylated alkaloid analogs.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group to hydroxyl groups on THIQ/benzylisoquinoline intermediates, generating methylated alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled construction of methylated THIQ alkaloid natural products and derivatives in one-pot cascades.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro one-pot multienzyme cascades; use of cofactor supply system; product analysis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzymatic conditions; SAM cofactor supply addressed with recycling module.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted as one of two MTs with unique characteristics for modular in vitro cascades.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: SAM cost is a practical limitation without cofactor recycling.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RnCOMT methylated THIQ intermediates in vitro. 2. Together with other MTs and SAM recycling enzymes, it enabled one-pot synthesis of methylated THIQ alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural product diversification and pharmacophore tailoring

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for selective methylation in multienzyme cascades producing diversified THIQ alkaloids; complements other O-methyltransferases.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates hydroxylated THIQ/benzylisoquinoline intermediates in one-pot biosynthetic cascades.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributed to efficient in vitro synthesis of methylated THIQ alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dependent on SAM or recycling systems.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MxSafC functioned with RnCOMT and other enzymes to diversify methylated THIQ products. 2. It expanded the one-pot biocatalytic route to methylated alkaloids.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Applicable in modular enzymatic cascades for selective O-methylation of THIQ intermediates; useful when assembling plant-like benzylisoquinoline pathways in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates hydroxyl groups on benzylisoquinoline intermediates downstream of NCS.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supported in vitro synthesis of methylated THIQ alkaloid natural products and derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cj-6-OMT was used as a methylation module in one-pot THIQ synthesis. 2. It helped generate methylated alkaloid products resembling plant pathway intermediates.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: SAM cofactor regeneration supporting benzylisoquinoline methylation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Practical cofactor-regeneration module to reduce SAM cost in scale-up of in vitro alkaloid methylation cascades; broadly useful in enzymatic alkaloid manufacturing.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Regenerates or supports supply of the methyl donor SAM for methyltransferase-dependent THIQ/benzylisoquinoline methylation reactions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled cost-effective one-pot methylation cascades for THIQ alkaloid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro modular cofactor supply system in one-pot cascades.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supports cofactor supply for target-product modification
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not part of alkaloid carbon skeleton synthesis directly, but explicitly highlighted as crucial for practical in vitro methylation because SAM cost otherwise limits scalability.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EcMAT and EcMTAN formed a modular SAM supply system. 2. This enabled practical one-pot methyltransferase cascades for THIQ alkaloid synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports scalable enzymatic production of methylated alkaloids

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Offers an alternative to NCS-based routes because it does not require the isoquinoline C6-hydroxyl motif; useful for producing highly modified phenylisoquinoline/THIQ derivatives inaccessible to standard cascades.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces dihydroisoquinoline precursors to chiral tetrahydroisoquinolines, which can then be N-methylated to yield alkaloid products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled efficient synthesis of phenylisoquinoline derivatives difficult to access in NCS-based systems, with near-quantitative yields in the reviewed examples.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multienzyme cascade biocatalysis; chemically synthesized precursor feeding; product analysis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor to target product
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes IRED tolerates highly modified DHIQ precursors unlike NCS-dependent entry routes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Requires chemically synthesized DHIQ precursors in the reviewed system.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IR45 reduced modified DHIQ precursors to chiral THIQs. 2. This route bypassed substrate limitations of NCS and enabled difficult phenylisoquinoline products.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for final N-methylation of THIQ scaffolds in modular enzyme cascades, increasing similarity to medicinal isoquinoline alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group to the nitrogen of tetrahydroisoquinoline intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completed efficient synthesis of N-methylated isoquinoline natural products and derivatives downstream of IR45 reduction.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CNMT methylated THIQ nitrogen atoms after IR45 reduction. 2. It enabled near-quantitative formation of N-methylated isoquinoline products in vitro.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A prime engineering target for producing difficult protoberberine alkaloids de novo in yeast; structure-guided mutagenesis can reprogram regioselectivity and substrate selectivity for customized alkaloid portfolios.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group to the 9-hydroxyl position of (S)-scoulerine and related tetrahydroprotoberberine intermediates in the berberine pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Engineered TfS9OMT mutants enabled de novo production of (S)-tetrahydropalmatine and (S)-tetrahydropalmatrubine in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Crystal structure analysis; mutagenesis; heterologous yeast pathway engineering; product detection of target alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms downstream modified intermediates leading to target products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review emphasizes structure-guided engineering to alter regioselectivity and substrate selectivity.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Wild-type regioselectivity/substrate scope may not be optimal for desired compounds; engineering needed.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key tailoring step; not stated as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TfS9OMT is an essential enzyme in berberine natural product biosynthesis. 2. Structure-guided mutants changed regioselectivity/substrate selectivity. 3. Engineered mutants enabled de novo yeast production of (S)-tetrahydropalmatine and (S)-tetrahydropalmatrubine.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical target for heterologous pathway reconstruction; overcoming its expression and activity bottleneck in E. coli is essential for industrial THPB production.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively converts tetrahydrobenzylisoquinoline substrates such as reticuline-type intermediates to the berberine bridge-containing scaffold leading to scoulerine/protoberberine products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as the bottleneck/essential enzyme for reconstructing tetrahydroprotoberberine biosynthesis in heterologous microbes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression engineering in E. coli; source screening; codon optimization; protein tagging; promoter optimization; pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Microbial host context strongly affected function; E. coli expression required extensive optimization.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review explicitly describes BBE as the rate-limiting enzyme in the THPB biosynthetic pathway and notes that lack of function in E. coli had blocked pathway reconstruction until recent engineering work.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Poor function in E. coli without extensive engineering; heterologous compatibility is a major barrier.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BBE is the key rate-limiting enzyme of the tetrahydroprotoberberine pathway. 2. Its lack of function in E. coli long blocked pathway reconstruction. 3. Screening, codon optimization, tagging, and promoter engineering enabled biosynthesis of scoulerine-derived products in E. coli.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal protoberberine alkaloids and pathway intermediates

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Enables microbial production of bisbenzylisoquinoline alkaloids when combined with coupling enzymes; important for expanding from monomeric to dimeric BIA production in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes epimerization steps in benzylisoquinoline alkaloid metabolism needed before oxidative coupling to bisbenzylisoquinoline products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Allowed construction of engineered yeast strains producing bisbenzylisoquinoline alkaloids such as guattegaumerine and berbamunine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional testing in yeast; strain engineering; fermentation optimization.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor configuration for target product
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named in the review as functioning in yeast to enable epimerization before dimerizing coupling.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PbDRS-DRR functioned in yeast. 2. It enabled epimerization of benzylisoquinoline alkaloids needed for bisbenzylisoquinoline formation. 3. Together with BsCYP80A1 it supported production of guattegaumerine and berbamunine.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key oxidative coupling enzyme for microbial bisbenzylisoquinoline production; a target for protein and strain engineering to access dimeric BIAs.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively couples two benzylisoquinoline alkaloid monomers to form bisbenzylisoquinoline alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled engineered yeast production of bisbenzylisoquinoline alkaloids such as guattegaumerine and berbamunine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional testing in yeast; strain engineering; protein engineering; fermentation optimization.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely demands careful host optimization because P450 function in yeast can be limiting, though the review does not detail drawbacks.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BsCYP80A1 enabled coupling of benzylisoquinoline monomers in yeast. 2. This was a breakthrough for de novo bisbenzylisoquinoline production. 3. Guattegaumerine and berbamunine were produced after strain and process optimization.
